title: An Ordinary Village Morning
body: Morning smoke hangs low over the village. Your hands still ache from yesterday's chores, and the day ahead promises nothing grand: water to carry, tools to mend, herbs to sort, and a body that will stay weak unless you make it useful. Before the road can become a story, the village is still your whole world: mud, iron, bitter medicine, shrine ash, and familiar voices.

choices.label: Walk through the village square and greet the people already awake
Carry stones at the river before chores
Help Blacksmith Guo mend farm tools

recordNpcConversation.topic: Helped him mend farm tools and learned that bad iron complains before it fails.

choices.label: Sort bitter herbs for Aunt Lin

recordNpcConversation.topic: Sorted bitter herbs for her and learned that medicine and poison can look nearly identical.

title: The Village Wakes
body:
The square wakes in layers. Old Ren sits with his cane across his knees, pretending not to watch every stance in sight. Aunt Lin ties bundles of herbs with red cord. Blacksmith Guo carries a repaired hinge still warm from the forge. Shrine Keeper Mei sweeps ash into a line so neat it feels like a lesson.

You slow near the well, listening as small rumors move from mouth to mouth: a broken wagon, frightened traders, scorch marks on the south road.

"Everyone keeps looking south," you say. "What happened out there?"

choices.label: Ask Old Ren why the caravan guards look nervous

recordNpcConversation.topic: Warned you that the south road feels wrong: frightened guards, bad orders, and danger in the hills.

choices.label: Ask Aunt Lin what medicine the traders requested

recordNpcConversation.topic: Said the traders needed burn salve and pain medicine, and that clean round burns are not bandit work.

choices.label: Ask Blacksmith Guo about the broken wagon axle

recordNpcConversation.topic: Showed you axle iron burned too cleanly for ordinary damage.

choices.label: Ask Shrine Keeper Mei what the incense smoke warned her about

recordNpcConversation.topic: Told you the shrine bell rang by itself in the night, thin as thread from the south.

choices.label: Take the gathered rumors toward the south road
Return home before choosing where to help

title: Old Ren Watches The Road
body:
You stop beside Old Ren's stool and nod toward the nervous caravan guards.

"You used to walk those roads. Are they afraid of bandits?"

Old Ren taps his cane against the packed dirt.

"Caravan guards get nervous for three reasons: bad pay, bad orders, or something in the hills that makes bad men act like prey. The south road has all three smells today."

"So I should stay away?"

"No. If you go, keep your ribs guarded and listen before you swing."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Old Ren's warning

title: Aunt Lin's Bitter Warning
body:
You carry a bundle of bitterleaf to Aunt Lin's table and lower your voice.

"The traders came to you first. What did they need?"

Aunt Lin does not look up from her herbs.

"Fever powder, burn salve, and something to keep a child quiet through pain."

"That sounds worse than a broken wagon."

She cuts a root too sharply.

"Bandits make bruises. Clean round burns are not bandit work."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Aunt Lin's warning

title: Guo's Broken Axle
body:
You find Guo beside the forge, turning a sliver of axle iron in his fingers.

"Can you fix it?"

He shows you the break: too smooth, too hot, as if a tiny sun passed through the metal and kept walking.

"I can fix a wheel. I cannot fix whatever made this."

"Then what does the road need?"

He closes his fist around the iron.

"More than a repaired wheel."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Guo's warning

title: Mei Counts The Smoke
body:
You pause at the shrine while Mei watches incense drift sideways though the morning air is still.

"Is that a bad sign?"

"A sign is only a sign. Fear makes it larger. Pride makes it useless."

"And what do you make of it?"

"The shrine bell rang once in the night without a hand touching it. The sound came from the south, thin as thread. Trouble is walking toward the immortal world, and mortals are already in its footprints."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Mei's warning

title: Cold Stones
body: The river does not care about ambition. It numbs your calves, steals your breath, and makes every stone heavier than it looked from shore. By the time you stagger back, your arms shake, but your feet remember how not to slip.

choices.label: Return to the village square and hear what people are saying

title: Honest Iron
body:
Guo gives you a cracked hoe, not a sword.

"I was hoping for something sharper," you say.

"Then learn not to ruin dull things first."

You pump the bellows, hold the tongs, and learn that bad iron complains before it fails. When a boy runs past the forge shouting about a broken wagon on the south road, Guo's hammer stops mid-swing.

choices.label: Return to the village square and ask what happened

title: Leaves That Look Alike
body:
Aunt Lin makes you sort roots until your fingertips smell bitter.

"These two look the same," you complain.

"Then look again. Most people die because they cannot tell medicine from poison until too late."

You swallow the answer you wanted to give her. Then a wounded mule limps into view, dragging half a harness and panic behind it.

choices.label: Return to the village square with the medicine satchel

title: Trouble On The South Road
body: At first it sounds like a normal road accident: a snapped axle, frightened traders, someone crying for help. Then you see the scorch mark on the dirt. It is too clean, too round, and still smoking without flame.

choices.label: Step into the broken trade road

title: Ten Days Before The Mountain Gate
body: The Azure Cloud Sect will open its mountain gate in ten days. Yesterday, immortals were only rumor. Now the road has left proof in your hands: jade, blood, cloud-iron, whispers, or a black-thread token. If you mean to survive the chance that found you, your mortal foundation has to become worth something quickly.

choices.label: Train your body at the river stone ford
Practice mortal martial arts in the abandoned threshing yard
Study herbs and crude alchemy with the village herbalist
Work the village forge and prepare road gear
Spend a quiet day with family, shrine, and road preparations
Seek guidance from the village teachers
Leave for the Azure Cloud mountain gate now
Ask whether your current qi is enough for the sect exam

title: Not Enough Qi
body:
The token warms when you think of the mountain, but your body answers with only a thin, uncertain heat. Mei watches your breathing. Aunt Lin watches your hands. Old Ren watches your stance.

"You can still go," Mei says, "but do not mistake being chosen for being ready. The sect mountain is rich with qi. If your body has not been tempered enough to endure it, the first trial may do the tempering for you. Painfully."

Aunt Lin snorts.

"Get more rough qi in your bones before you run toward immortals."

choices.label: Return to preparation

title: Village Teachers
body: The village has no immortals, but it has people who know what breaks mortals. Old Ren watches the practice yard with a cane across his knees. Aunt Lin sorts bitter roots in the shade. Blacksmith Guo hammers iron by sound. Shrine Keeper Mei sweeps ash from a cracked altar and counts every breath.

choices.label: Ask Old Ren to judge your martial stance

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked him to judge your martial stance before the sect exam.

choices.label: Help Aunt Lin prepare exam medicine

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked to help prepare medicine for the sect exam.

choices.label: Ask Blacksmith Guo for an honest forge lesson

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked him for a forge lesson and a practical road preparation.

choices.label: Ask Shrine Keeper Mei to teach you breath counting

recordNpcConversation.topic: Asked her to teach breath counting before the mountain trial.

choices.label: Step back before the trouble swallows you

title: Old Ren's Cane
body:
Old Ren was only a caravan guard, not a cultivator, but half the village still walks around his yard when he is holding a cane. He looks at your feet first, then your shoulders, then the space your ribs forgot to protect.

"Before you copy a fist form, learn why a body falls. Immortals call their power qi. Mortals meet it first as pressure, pain, heat, and breath. Temper the body badly, and qi will only find better places to break you."

choices.label: Hold your stance while Old Ren tries to break it
Return to the village teachers

title: Correction By Cane
body:
Old Ren's cane taps your ankle, hip, ribs, and wrist. Each touch is humiliatingly small. Each one fixes a leak in your strength. When he finally swings for real, your body answers before your pride can flinch.

"That warmth in your bones is not cultivation," he says. "It is your flesh learning not to waste what passes through it."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Ren's Foundation Drills
body:
Old Ren breaks your stance with one bored push. Then he makes you rebuild it from the feet up: heel, knee, hip, breath, guard. No glory. Just the parts you skipped.

"Do not chase qi," he says. "Let the body become something qi cannot immediately ruin."

choices.label: Return after more practice
Return to the village square

title: Aunt Lin's Bitter Table
body: Aunt Lin does not ask whether you want to become immortal. She asks whether you can tell fever leaf from throatbane after both have dried brown. Her table is covered in roots, powders, and consequences.

choices.label: Prepare a stable fever powder under Aunt Lin's eye
Return to the village teachers

title: Medicine That Leaves Less Debt
body:
The powder settles pale and even. Aunt Lin sniffs it, says nothing for a very long moment, then slides a crude qi elixir toward you.

"Good medicine is what helps without asking too much back."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke And Scolding
body: The batch smokes, then clumps, then earns a sound from Aunt Lin that is somehow worse than shouting. She makes you start again with easier herbs and explains exactly how impatience becomes poison.

choices.label: Return after studying herbs and heat
Return to the village square

title: Guo's Honest Iron
body:
Blacksmith Guo listens to metal more than people. He hands you a cracked sickle.

"Immortal swords can wait until you stop ruining mortal tools."

choices.label: Temper the cracked sickle without hiding the flaw
Return to the village teachers

title: The Ring Of Good Iron
body: The sickle rings dull but steady when Guo tests it. He nods once, which from him is nearly applause, and shows you how the same care can bind iron around a walking staff.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Crack Made Worse
body: You heat the sickle too eagerly. The crack widens with a tiny, accusing sound. Guo takes it back, breaks it clean, and makes you study the grain of the failure.

choices.label: Return after learning the forge rhythm
Return to the village square

title: Mei's Three Breaths
body:
Shrine Keeper Mei is younger than the title sounds and older than her face suggests. She sweeps ash into a neat line.

"The first immortal test is usually whether a mortal can stop performing long enough to breathe."

choices.label: Match three breaths to the incense smoke
Return to the village teachers

title: Smoke Without Trembling
body: The incense smoke rises straight. Your breath follows without chasing it. Mei smiles like someone watching a door unlock from the inside.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke That Shivers
body:
Your breath keeps trying to impress the shrine instead of match it. Mei lets you fail three times before speaking.

"Better here than on the mountain steps."

choices.label: Return after steadying your breath
Return to the village square

title: River Stone Ford
body: The village river has no spiritual pressure, no immortal formation, and no mercy. It is winter-cold around the knees and strong enough to drag a careless body downstream. The older youths trained here before they failed the sect exam. Their footprints are gone, but the stones remain.

choices.label: Carry river stones against the current
Hold horse stance beneath the little falls
Return to the village square

title: Cold Stone, Mortal Bone
body: You carry stones until your arms tremble and the current begins to feel like an opponent. Nothing mystical answers you. That is the point. Your body learns to continue without applause.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Sinews Like Wet Rope
body: Halfway across the ford your legs almost fold, then something changes. Your breath drops lower, your tendons stop screaming separately, and the river becomes one pressure instead of a hundred little pains.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Under The Little Falls
body: The falling water hammers your shoulders flat and steals heat from your skin. You stay in stance long enough for anger, fear, and ambition to become only breath.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Abandoned Threshing Yard
body: Dry grass grows through cracks in the old threshing yard. Children use it for dares, farmers use it to cut across the fields, and you use it because no one important comes here to laugh at mortal punches.

choices.label: Drill the village fist forms until dusk
Spar with the miller's sons
Practice staff basics with a bamboo pole
Return to the village square

title: Plain Fists
body: The forms are not beautiful. Step, guard, turn the hip, strike. Again. Again. By dusk the motions have less hesitation in them, and your knuckles remember the order better than your thoughts do.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Bruises Given And Taken
body: The miller's sons come at you laughing, then stop laughing when your guard holds. You still take hits, but you answer them. Mortal courage is not enough for immortals, perhaps, but it is not nothing.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Lesson In Bruises
body: You learn quickly that knowing a stance and keeping it while someone swings at your face are different skills. The yard sends you home sore, embarrassed, and slightly wiser.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Bamboo Distance
body: The bamboo pole flexes with every strike. You learn the comfort of distance: how a simple staff can make a stronger opponent spend one more breath reaching you.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Village Herbalist's Hut
body: The herbalist's hut smells of bitter roots, drying leaves, and smoke trapped in the rafters. There are no true immortal pills here, only mortal remedies, secondhand warnings, and a clay stove that cracks if treated like a sect furnace.

choices.label: Sort drying herbs by scent and vein
Boil a bitter road tonic without scorching it
Attempt a crude qi elixir with mortal tools
Return to the village square

title: Leaves That Look Alike
body: By afternoon your fingers are stained green and the herbalist has corrected you six times. The seventh time, you catch the mistake before she does. Her grunt of approval is almost a blessing.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Bitter Strength
body: The tonic tastes like boiled bark and regret, but it steadies your hands and warms your stomach. Mortal medicine will not open meridians, but it can keep a mortal body from failing early.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Clearer Crude Elixir
body:
The elixir is still crude by any sect standard, but the smoke thins at the right moment and the liquid settles without a sour edge. When a drop touches your tongue, heat moves under your skin instead of into your thoughts.

Aunt Lin watches your hands stop trembling.

"Do not call that cultivation. You are not gathering qi. You are letting a little of it temper the body before the sect teaches you how to breathe it properly."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke And Ambition
body:
The clay stove spits smoke, the herbs darken too quickly, and the finished elixir smells sharper than it should. When you taste it, heat claws through your muscles before settling into a rough, useful ache.

"That is why mortals should be careful with borrowed qi," Aunt Lin says. "It can temper a body. It can also leave dirt behind."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Village Forge Yard
body: The blacksmith cannot make flying swords. He can make hinges, ploughshares, nails, knives, and a staff that will not split on the first bad stone. The forge is mortal fire, but mortal fire still teaches discipline.

choices.label: Pump the bellows until your arms shake
Learn to read flaws in scrap iron
Reinforce a walking staff with iron bands
Return to the village square

title: Breath For The Forge
body: The bellows breathe through your arms. Too fast and the fire snaps wild; too slow and it sulks. By evening your shoulders ache, but you understand rhythm in a way lectures never taught.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Fault Lines
body: The blacksmith shows you where iron lies: dull color, wrong ring, a hairline crack waiting for strain. You begin to wonder how many people carry the same signs before they break.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Iron-Banded Staff
body:
The staff remains plain wood, but iron bands now guard the places most likely to split. The blacksmith tests it against the anvil and nods once.

"Do not trust anything that looks too fine for honest use."

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Family Courtyard
body: The courtyard is swept clean, though dust keeps finding its way back. Your family understands rain, debt, harvest, sickness, and hunger. They do not understand sects, except that people leave for them and return changed, if they return.

choices.label: Share a last ordinary meal and listen instead of boasting
Burn incense at the village shrine
Pack for the road and sleep early
Return to the village square

title: Last Ordinary Meal
body: The meal is simple enough to hurt: rice stretched with greens, pickled roots, tea poured twice from the same leaves. No one says that immortality begins by leaving mortal hands behind.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: Smoke At The Shrine
body: The shrine statue is cracked, rain-worn, and probably nothing like a true immortal. Still, the incense smoke rises straight for a moment, and your breathing follows it.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: A Mortal Pack
body: You pack cloth wraps, dried grain, a needle, spare cord, and more humility than you expected. None of it looks heroic. All of it may matter before the mountain is done with you.

choices.label: Return to the village square

title: The Broken Trade Road
body: The south road should smell of mule sweat, tea bundles, and dust. Today it smells of split axles, cold ash, and fear. Merchants whisper about bandits, but their eyes keep sliding toward the hills as if the real danger is not standing on the road at all. A black thread is tied around one broken wagon spoke.

choices.label: Take up a staff and confront the bandits
Question merchants, guards, and frightened witnesses
Help repair wagons, harness, and road gear
Treat the wounded hidden in the roadside ditch
Return to the village square

title: Spears In The Dust
body: You follow boot prints past a burned waymarker. Men step from the scrub with stolen spears and road knives, but none of them look proud. Behind them, someone small is crying inside a covered cart.

opening: {enemy} spread across the road while a child sobs behind the cart canvas.
strike: You step inside a spear thrust and strike {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Dust jumps under your heel as your blow lands on {enemy} for {damage} damage.
focus: You force qi into your limbs and break the bandit line for {damage} damage.
enemyAttack: {enemy} answer with desperate, ugly strikes for {damage} damage.
victory: The last bandit drops his weapon and runs for the hill scrub.
defeat: A spear haft catches your ribs, and the bandits drag the cart deeper into dust.

title: The Missing Child
body: Inside the cart, a child in travel-stained silk clutches a cloud-marked jade pendant. They cannot be older than seven. When you cut the rope from their wrists, the pendant warms against your palm, and for one breath the air smells like rain over high mountains.

choices.label: Calm the child and send them back with the merchants

title: A Debt With No Name
body: The child will not give a family name, only a shaky promise that someone will remember. One merchant notices the jade and goes pale. Another mutters that no road bandit should dare touch a child carrying such a mark unless someone stronger made them do it.

choices.label: Search the abandoned road before returning home

title: Driven From The Road
body: The bandits fight like cornered dogs. You are forced back before you can reach the covered cart, but not before you see a black-thread token passed from one bandit to another. Their fear is not of you. That may matter later.

choices.label: Bind your bruises, then search what the bandits left behind

title: Whispers Beside Broken Wheels
body: You pour tea, listen more than you speak, and let frightened people contradict each other until the shape of the truth appears. A caravan guard saw a paper charm flare before the attack. A merchant heard the words mountain gate. A captured lookout says the bandits pay tribute to someone who wears blue cloud silk.

choices.label: Search the road for the object behind the rumors

title: The Manual In The Axle Box
body: You mend cracked wheel hubs, reset harness buckles, and patch spear cuts in leather. In a hidden axle box, wrapped in oilcloth, you find a field manual full of practical repairs for cloud-iron fittings. It is not a grand inheritance, but it is the kind of knowledge people kill to keep useful.

choices.label: Search the axle box again for what the manual was hiding

title: The Girl With Splintered Breath
body: The wounded girl is hidden under reeds beside the ditch, her silk sleeve dark with blood and her breathing uneven in a way you do not understand. She tries to tell you she is only a merchant's niece, then fails to hide the pale light moving under her skin. Your village medicine should not matter to someone like this, but pain has made her human enough.

choices.label: Steady her qi with herbs, water, and patient hands

title: A Name Not Given
body:
By dusk the girl's pulse no longer fights itself. She leaves before the village can ask questions, pausing only to press a pale lotus hairpin into your hand.

"I will remember this."

She does not give her name, which makes the promise heavier rather than lighter.

choices.label: Stay a little longer and search the abandoned road

title: What The Dust Kept
body:
The road finally grows quiet enough to search properly. Under torn cart canvas and broken axle wood, you find a cracked pill bottle, two spirit stones wrapped in cloth, and a bronze token tied with black thread.

The token bears a cloud over a mountain. When you turn it north, it warms against your palm.

"This was not dropped by accident," you whisper.

choices.label: Return home and ask what the token symbol means

title: The Token No One Wants To Touch
body:
Back in the village, the bronze token draws eyes and silence. No one wants to be the first to name it. Old Ren studies the thread. Aunt Lin sniffs the metal like medicine gone bad. Guo refuses to put it near the forge fire. Mei watches the cloud mark as if it is watching back.

"Someone in this village knows what this means," you say. "Please. I need the truth before it finds me first."

choices.label: Ask Old Ren about the black thread

recordNpcConversation.topic: Studied the black-threaded token and said it looked like a travel claim for an immortal road.

choices.label: Ask Aunt Lin why the metal smells strange

recordNpcConversation.topic: Warned that the token carries clean qi and is not ordinary bandit loot.

choices.label: Ask Blacksmith Guo about the cloud-over-mountain mark

recordNpcConversation.topic: Recognized the cloud-over-mountain mark from old caravan hardware bound for the Azure Cloud Sect.

choices.label: Ask Shrine Keeper Mei why the token warms toward the north

recordNpcConversation.topic: Said the token is answering the mountain and that the Azure Cloud Sect may answer back.

choices.label: Wait with the token after gathering everyone's clues

title: Old Ren Names The Thread
body:
Old Ren turns the token over with the tip of his cane instead of touching it.

"Black thread means a carried claim. Caravan folk used to say such tokens buy passage on roads mortals do not own. If bandits had this, someone gave them courage they did not earn."

"Can you read the symbol?"

"No. But I have seen guards stand straighter when that cloud mark was painted on a sealed crate."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Old Ren's clue

title: Aunt Lin Smells Clean Qi
body:
Aunt Lin holds the token near a bowl of steeping herbs. The surface of the tea ripples before her hand moves.

"This is clean qi. Too clean for thieves. Too clean for anyone who still sweats for food."

"So it belongs to an immortal?"

"Or to someone who wants an immortal to come looking."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Aunt Lin's clue

title: Guo Remembers The Mark
body:
Guo sketches the cloud-over-mountain mark in soot on the forge stone.

"Saw this once on a crate axle bound north. The merchant would not say the sect name, but his guard did after too much wine. Azure Cloud."

"The Azure Cloud Sect?"

"If you have their token, either they lost something, or they are letting you carry it."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Guo's clue

title: Mei Watches The Token Breathe
body:
Mei lays the token beside the shrine incense. The smoke bends north without wind.

"It is answering the mountain. Quietly, but not weakly."

"Can the mountain answer back?"

"If this belongs to the Azure Cloud Sect, it may already be listening."

choices.label: Return to the village square with Mei's clue

title: The Immortal At The Door
body:
Three days pass. The token grows warmer each dawn.

On the fourth morning, the village dogs go silent. A blue-robed cultivator descends through the mist as if the road rose to meet his feet. He stops at your door and looks at the bronze token in your hand.

"So it chose a mortal after all. Fate is rarely polite. The Azure Cloud Sect opens its mountain gate in ten days. Bring the token. Climb if you dare."

You bow because your knees have already decided to bend.

"Why me?"

The immortal's smile is almost kind.

"Ask the mountain if you reach it."

choices.label: Begin preparing for the Azure Cloud mountain gate

title: A Chance From The Clouds
body: By nightfall the village is awake and afraid. The road trouble was not just bandits. Someone recognizes the cloud jade. Someone else has heard of blue-robed immortals gathering mortal candidates. Near midnight, a messenger arrives with a paper seal that refuses to burn in the cooking fire. The Azure Cloud Sect will test those with enough grit to climb. For the first time, the impossible has an address.

choices.label: Begin preparing for the Azure Cloud mountain gate

title: The Mountain Gate
body:
Mist coils around the ten thousand jade steps of the Azure Cloud Sect. Carriages crowd the lower road: lacquered clan wagons, merchant litters, hired guards holding parasols over children in silk. Your village pack suddenly feels louder than any boast.

A bronze bell sounds once. The exam has not begun, but judgment already has.

choices.label: Join the registration line with your trial token

title: Names Before The Gate
body:
A grey-robed examiner sits behind a jade table and checks each token without looking impressed by any of them. Rich candidates announce family names that make nearby servants bow. When your turn comes, you place the Azure Cloud trial token on the table.

The examiner's brush pauses.

"Village origin?"

"Yes."

A boy in blue silk laughs softly behind you.

"The mountain is generous this year."

choices.label: Keep your head level and answer the examiner plainly

recordNpcConversation.topic: Registered for the Azure Cloud Sect exam with the trial token while richer candidates watched.

title: Waiting Ground
body:
Candidates gather below the first stair. Some stretch in practiced silence. Some compare pills. Some pretend they are not afraid. The blue-silk boy keeps glancing at your patched cuffs as if poverty might spread by eye contact.

Rumors move through the crowd: the first trial crushes weak bodies, the second exposes shallow minds, the third has sent noble heirs home bleeding, and the elders keep a hidden score no one announces.

choices.label: Listen to the blue-silk heir mocking village candidates

recordNpcConversation.topic: He mocked your village origin before the first trial and treated the exam like inheritance.

choices.label: Speak with the quiet commoner counting breaths

recordNpcConversation.topic: She warned that the first stair tests whether fear can disrupt breath.

choices.label: Help the nervous merchant child secure his pack straps

recordNpcConversation.topic: Helped him secure his pack before the exam; he whispered that candidates sometimes vanish during the third trial.

choices.label: Step onto the first pressure stair

title: Silk And Teeth
body:
The blue-silk heir speaks loudly enough for half the waiting ground to hear.

"A village token. How rustic. Did the sect begin accepting lost farm tools as candidates?"

You look at his polished boots, then at the stair disappearing into mist.

"If the mountain wanted boots tested, yours would be climbing alone."

His smile thins. Good. He has learned your face.

choices.label: Return to the waiting ground

title: The Quiet Count
body:
The quiet girl breathes in sets of three. Her robe is plain, but the cloth is clean and patched with the same care Aunt Lin gives bandages.

"Do not let them set your pace," she says without opening her eyes. "The stair listens to panic."

"You sound like someone who has failed it before."

"My brother did. I listened when he came home."

choices.label: Return to the waiting ground

title: Loose Straps
body:
The merchant child fumbles with a pack nearly as large as his chest. You tighten one strap and shift the weight higher.

"Thank you," he says, face pale. "My father bought three advice scrolls. They all disagree."

"What do they agree on?"

"That the third trial is where polite people stop being polite."

choices.label: Return to the waiting ground

title: The First Trial Begins
body:
The examiner raises one hand. The jade steps answer with a pressure that rolls down the mountain like invisible rain. Some candidates gasp. Liu Zhen laughs too quickly. Han Yue lowers her shoulders and breathes.

The first trial is not a race. That does not stop half the crowd from trying to make it one.

choices.label: Climb steadily and conserve your breath
Rush ahead before the crowd can react

title: A Measured Ascent
body: You climb slowly, counting each breath. The pressure of the mountain presses against your bones, but your heart remains calm.

choices.label: Continue toward the first trial platform

title: Iron On The Steps
body: The reinforced staff bites against wet stone whenever your balance falters. It is only mortal iron and village workmanship, but under the mountain pressure that small certainty keeps your breathing even.

choices.label: Continue toward the first trial platform

title: A Reckless Ascent
body: You surge upward, earning surprised looks from the other hopefuls. The mountain pressure bites into your lungs, but for one bright moment you feel a thread of qi answer your ambition.

choices.label: Force yourself toward the first trial platform

title: The First Platform
body: A stone tablet waits beneath a bent pine. Its carved words shimmer faintly: To seek the Dao, first know the weight of desire.

choices.label: Trace the hidden qi current in the carved words
Place your palm on the tablet

title: A Hidden Current
body: The tablet's surface is cold, but beneath the characters you sense a patient current of qi circling like mist around a peak. You follow it for one breath and understand a fraction more than the others.

choices.label: Place your palm on the tablet

title: The First Trial
body:
The tablet does not teach gently. The qi of the Azure Cloud mountain pours through the stone and into your arms like cold rain forced through cracked earth. This is not the thin warmth of village elixirs. This is abundance, heavy enough to bruise.

Your mortal body panics first. Breath catches. Knees shake. Every careless day of training becomes a gap the mountain tries to split open.

Then Old Ren's stance holds your bones in line. Aunt Lin's warnings keep you from swallowing the power whole. Mei's breath count gives the storm a rhythm. Guo's lessons remind you that pressure can either break a flaw or close it.

Something in your flesh gives way. Something stronger answers.

You break through to Mortal Middle.

choices.label: Endure the breakthrough and let the tablet finish judging you

title: After The First Trial
body:
Cold light fades from the tablet. You are still standing. Several candidates are not.

Liu Zhen stares at you as if your breakthrough insulted him personally.

"Luck," he says.

Han Yue gives you the smallest nod.

Above the platform, Examiner Su marks your name with a second stroke instead of one.

"Mortal Middle during the first trial," he says. "Crude foundation. Unusual endurance. Continue."

choices.label: Walk toward the second trial mirrors

title: Second Trial: Cloud Mirror
body:
The next platform holds seven bronze mirrors filled with slow white cloud. Candidates step before them and see different things: family banners, dead parents, piles of pills, empty hands.

Examiner Su's voice carries over the platform.

"The mirror does not ask what you want. It asks whether you can see clearly while wanting it."

choices.label: Study the smallest flaw in the mirror's cloud pattern
Anchor your breath and refuse the images

title: Clear Cloud
body:
The mirror shows your village smaller than a thumbprint beneath the mountain. For a breath, shame tries to make it tiny. Then Guo's hammer, Aunt Lin's bitter table, Mei's ash line, and Old Ren's cane sharpen in your memory.

Small is not worthless. Small is where roots begin.

The cloud parts.

choices.label: Proceed to the survival ground

title: Cloud In The Lungs
body:
The mirror catches you with images of silk robes, laughing heirs, and your own hands empty at the village gate. Your breath stumbles. Then it returns, rough but yours.

The mirror does not open kindly, but it opens.

choices.label: Proceed to the survival ground

title: Third Trial: Mistwood Ground
body:
The third platform is not a platform at all. It is a mist-filled grove sealed by formation light. Examiner Su gestures toward the trees.

"Each candidate faces what the formation draws to them. Survive, retreat, or be carried out. Rank is not given to clean robes."

Somewhere in the fog, something growls.

choices.label: Enter the mistwood with your guard raised

title: First Breath Of The Dao
body: A thread of qi settles inside your meridians. When the light fades, a wooden token rests in your palm, and the first layer of the Azure Cloud Breathing Method echoes in your mind.

choices.label: Train the Azure Cloud Breathing Method under the pine

title: Breath Beneath The Pine
body: You kneel beneath the bent pine and follow the manual's first circulation route. The mountain pressure no longer feels like a wall. It feels like a teacher pressing one finger against your chest.

choices.label: Stand and present your circulation to the elders

title: Outer Disciple
body:
The watching elder gives the smallest nod. A path opens through the mist toward the outer sect courtyard. Behind you, the exam grounds continue judging the hopeful and the broken. Ahead, dormitories, rules, missions, rivals, and real cultivation wait.

Your village road has ended. Your sect road has begun.

choices.label: Follow the misty path toward the outer sect courtyard

title: Mist Wolf Ambush
body: The path narrows between old pines. A low growl rolls through the fog, and a pale spirit wolf steps from the roots with its hackles raised.

opening: Fog curls between the old pines as {enemy} lowers its head and claims the path.
Wet needles shift underfoot while {enemy} circles through the white mist.
strike: You drive forward over slick roots and strike {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Your blow cuts through drifting fog and catches {enemy} for {damage} damage.
You use a pine trunk to steady your footing, then hit {enemy} for {damage} damage.
focus: You pull qi through your breath until the mist trembles, then strike {enemy} for {damage} damage.
Cold fog beads on your sleeves as qi gathers in your arm and lands for {damage} damage.
The mountain pressure tightens around your ribs, but you force qi into the blow and deal {damage} damage.
enemyAttack: {enemy} springs from the fog and rakes across your guard for {damage} damage.
A pale shape flashes between the roots; {enemy} bites in for {damage} damage.
{enemy} vanishes behind a pine, then crashes into your side for {damage} damage.
victory: {enemy} stumbles in the wet grass, its growl fading into the fog.
The old pines fall silent as {enemy} collapses beneath the mist.
defeat: The fog swallows the path as {enemy} drives you back toward the trial stones.

title: Third Trial Blood
body:
The mist wolf collapses into the wet grass. Its spirit core flickers once in your palm before going still. Somewhere beyond the trees, the exam bell rings again.

You are bruised, breathing hard, and no longer able to pretend the sect tests are symbolic.

choices.label: Study the spirit core and steady your breath

title: Preparing The First Bottleneck
body:
The mist wolf core is too weak to force a realm breakthrough, but its fading qi teaches you how unstable power disperses. You mark that lesson in your heart for the day you challenge a true boundary.

At the edge of the grove, someone groans beneath a fallen branch. It is Qiao Min, the nervous merchant child, one arm pinned and his token just out of reach.

choices.label: Approach the hidden trial at the grove edge

title: Hidden Trial: The Pinned Candidate
body:
Formation mist curls around Qiao Min's trapped arm. Liu Zhen appears between the trees, robe torn but pride intact. He sees the fallen token and smiles.

"Leave him. The bell is nearly done. Help me reach the exit first and my family will remember your sense."

Qiao Min tries not to beg. That makes it worse.

choices.label: Free Qiao Min even if it costs your ranking

recordNpcConversation.topic: Freed him during the hidden character trial instead of chasing rank.

choices.label: Reject Liu Zhen but take the fastest safe path out
Accept Liu Zhen's offer and hurry for rank

recordNpcConversation.topic: Accepted his offer during the hidden trial, gaining advantage at a moral cost.

title: A Hand Under The Branch
body:
The branch shifts one finger-width at a time. Qiao Min bites through a cry. When he finally pulls free, the formation bell is already ringing the last count.

Examiner Su is waiting at the exit.

"Late," he says.

Then his gaze flicks to Qiao Min walking beside you.

"Noted."

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: No Bought Path
body:
You do not take Liu Zhen's bargain. You also do not throw your life away trying to solve every wound in the mist. The exit opens under your hand with seconds to spare.

Liu Zhen arrives after you, face pale with fury.

"Village luck again," he says.

"You should buy better luck."

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: A Clean Robe, A Dirty Hand
body:
Liu Zhen's shortcut works. You reach the exit early, but the mist clings to your sleeves with a cold that does not fade.

Examiner Su looks at you for one heartbeat too long.

"Efficient," he says.

It does not sound like praise.

choices.label: Stand before the ranking stone

title: Ranking Stone
body:
Names flare across the ranking stone in cloud-white light. Noble heirs search for their places first. Servants whisper. Failed candidates are led away with blank faces.

Your name appears lower than Liu Zhen's and higher than half the crowd expected. Beside it, a second mark blooms: First Trial Breakthrough.

Examiner Su reads the result aloud.

"Accepted as an outer disciple. Foundation rough. Endurance notable. Character under observation."

From somewhere near the elder seats, you feel attention settle on you like a hand between the shoulders.

choices.label: Receive the outer disciple token and first breathing method

title: Outer Sect Courtyard
body: Morning mist drifts between dormitories, training platforms, and the assignment hall. Outer disciples hurry past with manuals under one arm and sect task slips in the other. For the first time, the day is yours to spend.

choices.label: Train the Azure Cloud Breathing Method at the morning platform
Study Pine Shadow Step with the footwork disciples
Take a herb gathering task from the assignment hall
Rest in the outer disciple dormitory
Spend a free hour around the outer sect paths
Ask an elder about your first real bottleneck

title: Morning Breathing Platform
body: You sit among rows of new disciples and follow the Azure Cloud circulation route. Each breath draws a little more mountain qi into your meridians, though the effort leaves your body warm and heavy.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Pine Shadow Lesson
body: A senior outer disciple marks footwork patterns in the dust. Your first attempts are clumsy, but by dusk your steps begin to turn with the wind instead of against it.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Assignment Hall
body: The assignment hall smells of ink, wet wood, and ambition. A steward slides you a thin bamboo slip: gather frost-vein herbs from the lower grove before another disciple claims the task.

choices.label: Search the lower grove carefully
Rush the task before sunset

title: A Thread Beneath The Frost
body: You slow your breathing until the grove's cold qi separates into faint threads. One thread bends beneath a fallen root, revealing a second cluster of frost-vein herbs and an old pill bottle wrapped in oilcloth. The steward's eyes sharpen when you return with more than he expected.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Frost-Vein Herbs
body: You kneel beside a shaded stream and find the pale herbs where the steward said they would grow. The hall pays a modest reward, and someone has tucked a minor qi pill into the bundle.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Thorns Under Frost
body: Careful intent is not the same as trained eyes. You mistake pale thorns for herb stems and only notice when they tighten around your wrist. You return with enough herbs to pass the task, but the grove takes blood for the lesson.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Before The Bell
body: You move hard and clean through the lower grove, trusting your legs where another disciple might trust caution. By sunset your robe is torn and your lungs burn, but the steward cannot argue with a full bundle delivered before the bell.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: A Hasty Task
body: You return scratched, tired, and holding a smaller bundle than expected. The steward pays you anyway, but his raised eyebrow says the sect remembers careless work.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Outer Disciple Dormitory
body: Your assigned room is narrow, plain, and blessedly quiet. By morning your breathing has steadied and some of the strain has left your bones.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard

title: Outer Sect Paths
body: The outer sect is larger than the courtyard first promised. Stone paths wind between lecture halls, work yards, medicine plots, and quiet cliffs where new disciples pretend not to be homesick.

choices.label: Visit the scripture pavilion threshold
Help weed the medicine garden
Watch sparring at the blade-scarred arena
Walk to the cloud edge overlook
Return to the main courtyard

title: Scripture Pavilion Threshold
body: A grey-robed clerk guards the pavilion doors with a brush, a ledger, and the bored patience of someone who has refused a thousand ambitious disciples before breakfast. The open shelves near the entrance hold only copied fragments, but even fragments can cut open a mind.

choices.label: Copy breathing annotations from a worn manual
Ask the clerk which rules new disciples break first
Step back from the pavilion

title: Borrowed Ink
body: You copy until your wrist aches. Most of the notes are mundane reminders about posture and breath, but one line describes qi as weather inside the bones. The phrase follows you long after the ink dries.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: The Clerk's Warning
body:
The clerk does not look up from the ledger.

"Do not steal pills, do not duel near the kitchens, do not insult inner disciples, and never mistake borrowed sect property for destiny."

Then he pauses and marks your name neatly.

"Listening is rarer than talent."

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Medicine Garden Work
body: The medicine garden smells of wet soil and sharp green leaves. A senior sister points you toward a row of stubborn spirit weeds whose roots cling like little fists around the frost-vein herbs.

choices.label: Pull each weed slowly and preserve the roots
Finish quickly before your back gives out
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Roots Intact
body:
By sunset the senior sister inspects the row and gives a rare approving hum. She presses a minor qi pill into your palm.

"Cultivation also means learning which roots should not be torn."

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Dirt Under The Nails
body: You finish the row, but several frost-vein leaves droop in the aftermath. The senior sister pays the token stone for your labor and says nothing, which somehow weighs more than a scolding.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Blade-Scarred Arena
body: At the arena, older outer disciples trade blows on stone scored by years of practice weapons. Each exchange is quick, ugly, and practical. No one here moves like the songs say heroes move.

choices.label: Study how Pine Shadow Step is used under pressure
Focus on how the losers recover their guard
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Footwork In The Dust
body: The lesson is not in the winning strike. It is in the half-step before it, when one disciple lets the opponent's force pass through empty air. Your own feet remember the pattern before your mind can name it.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: After The Hit
body: A disciple takes a practice blade to the ribs, staggers, and still raises his guard before breathing again. The watching crowd laughs, but you understand the hidden lesson: survival often begins after pride has already lost.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Cloud Edge Overlook
body: Beyond the last pine, the mountain falls away into a white sea of cloud. The sect sounds grow distant here: bells softened by mist, training shouts reduced to memory, your own breath louder than either.

choices.label: Meditate until the clouds stop feeling separate from you
Watch the paths below for useful patterns
Return to the outer sect paths

title: Mist In The Meridians
body: You breathe with the slow movement of the cloud sea. For a few precious moments, your meridians loosen and the sect no longer feels like a place you must conquer. It feels like weather you might learn to survive.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Paths Beneath The Cloud
body: From above, the outer sect reveals its habits. Couriers avoid the kitchens at noon, senior disciples cut through the west arcade, and the quietest path to the assignment hall runs behind the medicine sheds.

choices.label: Return to the outer sect paths

title: Words Before The Bottleneck
body: The elder listens to your report and taps the mist wolf core with one finger. Minor stage breakthroughs may be forced with enough qi, but true realm bottlenecks demand timing, resources, and a foundation that will not crack under pressure.

choices.label: Return to the courtyard and keep preparing

title: Forced Back
body: You stagger away from the mist wolf and retreat to the tablet platform, wounded but alive. The mountain has shown you that cultivation is not only meditation.

choices.label: Return to the mountain gate and begin again
